status_notes: Initial research from AWS KMS documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs) and partial Pass 2 (API). Needs Pass 3 (cross-reference) and Pass 5 (human review with live AWS environment).
notes: Foundation encryption service — most AWS services use KMS for encryption at rest and in transit. FIPS 140-3 Level 3 validated HSMs. Global service with regional key storage.

match_nodes.description: Keys you create, own, and manage

data.description: KMS keys you create and control. Full control over lifecycle, policies, rotation, deletion. Monthly fee + per-use fees.

options.name: Symmetric AES-256-GCM
options.description: 256-bit AES-GCM symmetric encryption key. Most common. Used for envelope encryption.

options.name: RSA 2048
options.description: Asymmetric RSA 2048-bit key for encryption or signing

options.name: RSA 3072
options.description: Asymmetric RSA 3072-bit key for encryption or signing

options.name: RSA 4096
options.description: Asymmetric RSA 4096-bit key for encryption or signing

options.name: ECC P-256
options.description: Elliptic curve P-256 key for signing

options.name: ECC P-384
options.description: Elliptic curve P-384 key for signing

options.name: ECC P-521
options.description: Elliptic curve P-521 key for signing

options.name: ECC secp256k1
options.description: Elliptic curve secp256k1 key for signing (blockchain compatible)

default.note: Symmetric AES-256 if key spec not specified.

match_nodes.description: Keys created by AWS services in your account

data.description: Keys created automatically by AWS services (alias aws/service). Service-controlled. Cannot modify policies or manually rotate. Automatically rotated annually (since May 2022). No monthly fee.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use customer managed keys when you need control over key policy, rotation schedule, or cross-account access.

data.description: JSON policies attached directly to KMS keys. Primary mechanism for controlling who can use and manage the key. Every KMS key has exactly one key policy.

default.note: Default key policy allows the root account full access and enables IAM policies to grant KMS permissions.

audit.console: KMS -> Customer managed keys -> {key} -> Key policy

data.description: Temporary permissions on KMS keys without changing the key policy. Used by AWS services to encrypt/decrypt on your behalf. Grant tokens provide immediate access before eventual consistency.

audit.console: KMS -> Customer managed keys -> {key} -> Key policy -> Grants

data.description: Automatic annual rotation of the backing key material. Old key material preserved for decryption. The key ID, ARN, and alias don't change. Only available for symmetric customer managed keys with KMS-generated material.

default.note: Enabled (annual, 365 days) for AWS managed keys.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable automatic rotation for all customer managed symmetric keys.

audit.console: KMS -> Customer managed keys -> {key} -> Key rotation

data.description: Controls whether KMS generates key material or you provide it.

options.name: KMS-generated
options.description: Default. Key material generated by KMS.

options.name: Imported
options.description: You import key material into a KMS key.

options.name: CloudHSM
options.description: Key material generated in your CloudHSM cluster.

options.name: External key store
options.description: Key material held in your external key manager (XKS).

default.note: Key material generated by KMS unless otherwise specified.

data.description: KMS keys that can be replicated into multiple regions. Same key material in each region. Enables cross-region encrypt/decrypt without cross-region API calls.

Key-value pairs included as additional authenticated data (AAD) in cryptographic operations. Not encrypted but bound to the ciphertext — must provide the same context to decrypt. Logged in CloudTrail.
data.vendor_recommendation: Always use encryption context for envelope encryption. Include meaningful context (table name, user ID) to limit ciphertext reuse.

match_nodes.description: Scheduled key destruction with waiting period

data.description: Keys cannot be instantly deleted. Deletion requires a waiting period (7-30 days) during which the key is disabled. Can be cancelled during waiting period.

audit.cli: aws kms describe-key --key-id {id} (check KeyState and DeletionDate)

data.description: Human-readable names for KMS keys (alias/my-key). Can be updated to point to different keys. Enable key rotation without changing references in code. AWS managed keys use alias/aws/service-name.
